name: Sahad Muhammed Villan
intro: Machine Learning Engineer building medical imaging and surgical AI systems.
aboutLead: I turn medical imaging research into systems that can be tested, explained, and deployed.
aboutBody: My work spans PyTorch, computer vision, uncertainty estimation, deployment, and medical hardware integration.

projects.title: OR Lens
projects.summary: Evaluation and failure analysis for surgical video models.

projects.title: Surgical Tissue Localization & Deformation Tracking
projects.summary: CoTracker3 tissue tracking with uncertainty, drift, and strain analysis.
projects.videoLabel: Surgical GPS tissue and instrument tracking preview

projects.title: Brain Tumor Detection & Segmentation
projects.summary: ResNet and ResUNet workflow for MRI detection and segmentation.

projects.title: Interactive MRI Volume Segmentation
projects.summary: SAM and XMem tool for propagating MRI masks through a volume.

projects.title: Blood Cell Detection
projects.summary: Deformable DETR experiments focused on small platelet detection.

experience.role: Master Thesis Student, Medical Imaging & AI
highlights: Built a PyTorch pipeline for tissue oxygen saturation estimation, reducing MAE by 25 to 30%.
Added MC Dropout uncertainty estimation with over 92% calibrated coverage and SHAP-based explanations.
Packaged the workflow with Docker and Azure ML and delivered a clinician-facing GUI.

experience.company: ICCAS, Innovation Center Computer Assisted Surgery
highlights: Developed Capsule Network approaches for hyperspectral image segmentation.
Benchmarked robustness under noise, camera motion, and illumination changes.
Introduced GitHub Actions checks for a more reproducible development workflow.
Developed quantitative OpenCV sensor-processing pipelines for diagnostic hardware.
Built low-latency FastAPI and Docker services for point-of-care assay readers.
Automated feature localization with OpenCV and supported sensor calibration.
Contributed to verification, validation, and technical documentation across the device lifecycle.
